names.japanese_name: アゾジカルボンアミド
names.japanese_label_name: アゾジカルボンアミド

natural_occurrence.description: A dough conditioner and flour bleaching agent. Infamous as the 'yoga mat chemical' because it's also used to make foamed plastics!

manufacturing.description: Synthesized by oxidation of biurea or by reaction of hydrazine with phosgene and ammonia.

other_uses.industrial: Foaming agent for plastics, yoga mats, shoe soles, synthetic leather
other_uses.household: Used in manufacturing yoga mats and flip-flops!

regulatory_history.event: FDA approves azodicarbonamide as flour treatment agent
WHO warns it may cause asthma in workers exposed to ADA dust
Subway removes ADA from bread after public outcry about 'yoga mat chemical'
Many US food manufacturers phase out ADA voluntarily

commonly_found_in: Some US bread and baked goods (declining use)

adi_context.source_note: No ADI established. This is the infamous YOGA MAT CHEMICAL! It's used to make foamed plastics and also as a dough conditioner. Banned in EU and Australia, but legal in USA and Canada. Subway famously removed it in 2014 after massive public backlash.
